name: Name

light.biasTip: Depth bias — increase (toward 0) if shadows detach, decrease if you see acne
light.areaTip: Half-size of the area the shadow covers. Smaller = sharper shadows over a smaller region.

camera.sizeTip: Half-height of the orthographic view volume
camera.shadowHint: Lights only cast shadows if their own "Cast shadows" is on. Per-object cast/receive is set on each model's transform.
camera.envHint: Scales ambient light from the environment map in the output and viewer. Lower for more directional contrast; 0 lights the model with scene lights only.

feed.description: Renders the data-channel fields visible to this node (global + this node as a set_data scope target) through the template below.

avatar.defaultExpressionHint: Resting expression weights held until a blendshape broadcast (VMC, lipsync, tracking) overrides them.

scene.broadcastHint: How often the server merges pose + blendshape sources and broadcasts a frame. Default 60. Lower values reduce bandwidth at the cost of smoothness.

emptyState.selectNode: Select a node to edit its properties.
emptyState.selectLayer: Select a layer to edit its properties.
emptyState.graphsTab: Editing a graph — select nodes on the canvas.
emptyState.graphsTabNoGraph: Select or create a logic graph to start editing.

pickButton: Pick from the asset library in the bottom dock

material.noModel: Load a model to edit its materials.
material.toonHint: MToon is the toon look (ignores environment light). PBR responds to scene lights and the camera's environment intensity — switch to PBR for full light falloff and darkness.
material.apbrTip: Advanced PBR (MeshPhysicalMaterial): specular, clearcoat, sheen, transmission…
material.resetTip: Drop overrides and rebuild this material from the VRM file

calibration.headLabel: Head — look straight ahead, relax neck
calibration.leftArmLabel: Left arm — touch index finger to left eye corner
calibration.rightArmLabel: Right arm — touch index finger to right eye corner
calibration.pipelineError: Could not reach pipeline — is tracking active?
calibration.hint: Head: relax into your natural posture. Arms: touch fingertip to eye corner, hold steady.

vmc.localIpsHeader: This machine's IPs — point your capture app here
vmc.compatHint: Works with RhyLive, VSeeFace, VTube Studio, and any VMC-compatible app. Set your capture app's OSC output to the IP above and port {{port}}.
vmc.mapperVisualJson: Switch between visual editor and raw JSON

lipsync.vowelCalibHint: Hold each button while sustaining the vowel sound (~1s).

mediapipe.headTorsoHint: Head/torso — capture in a relaxed neutral stance.
mediapipe.fingersHint: Fingers — hold one hand up in the avatar's finger rest pose (for VRoid: straight fingers, thumb relaxed-out). The higher hand wins; the other side is mirrored from it.
mediapipe.pipelineError: Could not reach pipeline — is tracking active?
mediapipe.ikHint: When off, arms are driven by per-bone quaternions (lower fidelity but always stable).
mediapipe.headCalibNote: (gains amplify rotation axes; rest pitch shifts neutral nod)

help.transform: Learn about transform, position, and scene hierarchy
help.avatar: Learn how to load and configure VRM avatars
help.expressions: Learn how to set default expressions on your avatar
help.materials: Learn how to override avatar materials and shaders
help.animation: Learn about idle animations and blend timing
help.camera: Learn about camera properties and projections
help.light: Learn about light types and shadow settings
help.breathing: Learn how the breathing behavior works
help.compose: Learn about compose layers and output compositing
help.cameraEffects: Opens the doc section for this specific effect
help.propImage: Learn about image planes and billboards
help.propVideo: Learn about video planes and playback options
help.propAudio: Learn about audio sources and spatial sound
help.propText: Learn about text nodes and rendering options
help.propFeed: Learn about in-scene feed overlays and data channels
help.camFov: Vertical angle of the camera's view cone in degrees — lower values zoom in and compress depth, higher values show more of the scene
help.camProjection: Perspective maps 3D depth naturally; Orthographic renders everything at the same size regardless of distance — use it for flat overlays
help.camClipping: Near and far are the depth planes between which the camera renders — objects outside this range are invisible
help.camEnv: Multiplier (0–2) on how strongly the HDRI environment map lights PBR/MToon materials; does not affect the background image
help.lightType: Selects how the light emits — Point radiates from a position, Directional casts parallel rays like sunlight, Ambient adds constant fill, Spot emits a cone
help.lightIntensity: How bright the light is; 1 is the default, 0 is off, values above 1 overexpose nearby surfaces
help.lightShadows: Enables shadow rendering for this light — requires the camera's shadow setting to also be on
help.matMode: Shader model: MToon is toon shading (ignores scene lights), PBR responds to lights and environment, APBR adds advanced lobes like clearcoat and glass
help.matEmissive: Emissive color makes the surface appear self-lit; Emissive intensity multiplies it — values above 1 produce a bright glow when Bloom is active
help.matMetalRough: Roughness (0=mirror, 1=diffuse) controls sharpness of reflections; Metalness (0=non-metal, 1=conductor) changes how the surface reflects light
help.matEnv: Per-material multiplier on the scene's environment map contribution; at 0 this material ignores the HDRI entirely
help.matAdvanced: APBR-only lobes: clearcoat, sheen, transmission, iridescence, anisotropy — each defaults to 0 and renders identically to PBR until raised
help.partEmission: How many particles spawn per second; enable Burst Mode to fire all particles at once instead
help.partLifetime: How long each particle lives before disappearing; Lifetime ± adds randomness so they don't all vanish at the same time
help.partSize: Base sprite size in world units and per-particle random variation; Size Over Lifetime controls whether sprites shrink, grow, or pulse as they age
help.partColor: Start and end colors that each particle interpolates between over its life; Alpha and Alpha Over Lifetime control overall transparency and fade curves
help.partDirection: Primary travel direction vector and cone spread angle; Speed and Speed ± control how fast particles move
help.partOrigin: Half-extents of the spawn box around the emitter — all zero means a single-point emitter, larger values spread the birth positions across a volume
help.partMotion: Constant acceleration applied each frame (gravity); Turbulence adds noise-based random velocity perturbation
help.partRotation: Free mode gives each particle a random spin; Velocity mode aligns the sprite's top edge with its travel direction
help.partRendering: Blend mode controls how particles composite over the scene (additive for glows, normal for smoke); Max Count sets the pool size; Depth Write/Test control z-buffer interaction
help.videoPlayback: Controls when and how the video plays — Autoplay, Loop, what happens On End, and the audio volume
help.videoChroma: Removes a specific background color from the video to composite green-screen or blue-screen footage directly in the scene
help.audioType: Simple plays at the same volume everywhere; Directional falls off with distance and can be shaped into a 3D cone
help.audioSpatial: Distance and cone parameters for directional audio — reference distance, rolloff, max distance, and the cone angles
